summary: redis-type-os: typosquat of redis-om with no code, pulls suspicious check-ulid transitive
details: Package name `redis-type-os` is a transposition of the legitimate `redis-om` package. `package.json` impersonates the real redis-om maintainer (`Guy Royse <guy@guyroyse.com>`) and points `repository` and `homepage` at `redis/redis-om-node`; README, CHANGELOG, and docs are copied from redis-om. The tarball ships no code: declared `main: dist/index.js` and `types:./dist/index.d.ts` do not exist in the published artifact. The package's only on-install effect is dependency resolution — `dependencies` declares `check-ulid@^3.0.2`, itself a name resembling the legitimate `ulid` package, which is silently pulled into the installer's dependency tree on `npm install`. The combination of stolen author identity, copied documentation, missing advertised code, and a sibling-typosquat dependency is a dependency-graph poisoning pattern: the lure package is hollow, the harm is delegated to the transitive `check-ulid`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
